channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: How to build your first MCP server in 10 minutes
item.description: I built my first MCP server last week and it was way simpler than I expected. Here is exactly how, no fluff. Prerequisites Node.js 20+ 10 minutes Step 1: Scaffold npx create-mcp-server my-first-server cd my-first-server npm install This generates a complete TypeScript project with one example tool....
item.dc:creator: GrahamduesCN

item.title: The commented-out code was a to-do, not a bug: finishing waves in SignalK's Open-Meteo plugin
item.description: Our boat's weather comes from a chain we didn't fully own. Open-Meteo serves a free forecast; the SignalK Open-Meteo provider plugin pulls it into the boat's own weather API at /signalk/v2/api/weather/forecasts/point; and our weather-mcp reads that — so the voice agent gets sea state from the...
item.dc:creator: Bryan Clark

item.title: Intellibooks Explains Data Ownership Roles: Building Strong Data Governance for Enterprise AI
item.description: How Intellibooks Helps Organizations Define Data Owner, Data Custodian, and Data Steward Responsibilities As organizations accelerate their AI transformation, data has become their most valuable enterprise asset. Whether you're implementing Generative AI, Agentic AI, Machine Learning, or enterprise...
item.dc:creator: Intellibooks AI

item.title: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기
item.description: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기 이커머스, 출석체크, 메뉴판, 결제링크 — QR 필요한 곳 어디든 프로젝트에 qr code generator 기능이 필요한데 직접 구현하기엔 시간이 아까운가요? REST API 한 줄이면 됩니다. Quick Start # 즉시 테스트 (API 키 불필요) curl https://api.lazy-mac.com/qr-code/ 실전 예제 Python import requests resp =...
item.dc:creator: 2x lazymac

item.title: How Bonnard Builds Agent-Friendly MCPs
item.description: Exposing your data over MCP is the easy part. Designing a tool an agent uses well is the hard part. An agent can only use a tool it can read, so the work is shaping the tool for how the model calls it, not just for the human looking at the result. These are the techniques behind @bonnard/mcp-charts...
item.dc:creator: Max Mealing

item.title: How to Connect an AI Agent to Your Data Warehouse
item.description: Most teams connecting AI agents to their data warehouse start with text-to-SQL. The agent generates SQL from natural language, runs it against the warehouse, and returns results. It works until it doesn't: hallucinated JOINs, inconsistent aggregations, no access control, no audit trail. There's a...
item.dc:creator: Max Mealing

item.title: Two agent-tool attacks, one lesson: detection has a ceiling, enforceable authority has a floor
item.description: Two agent-tool security papers landed in June. Read together, they expose the boundary between semantic detection and enforceable control. A common response to malicious agent tools is to scan tool descriptions: inspect the text an agent is about to trust, decide whether it appears malicious, and...
item.dc:creator: Michael "Mike" K. Saleme

item.title: Indonesia UBO checks after the AML clampdown
item.description: Two Indonesian shareholders with the same name appeared in one acquisition structure. The legacy paid database collapsed them into a single beneficial owner because both addresses had been cut down to district level. AHU filings showed different NIK-linked records and different appointment dates....
item.dc:creator: OpenRegistry

item.title: What Is an Agentic Semantic Layer?
item.description: An agentic semantic layer is a metadata layer between AI agents and a data warehouse that defines business metrics, enforces access control, and exposes governed query interfaces. Instead of writing raw SQL, agents query metric definitions through protocols like MCP (Model Context Protocol) or REST...
item.dc:creator: Max Mealing

item.title: Why Your AI Agents Need a Semantic Layer
item.description: Give an AI agent access to your data warehouse and it will write SQL. It might even write good SQL. But ask two agents the same revenue question and you'll get two different numbers. Neither matches finance's report. That's the core problem: text-to-SQL gives agents access to data, not...
item.dc:creator: Max Mealing

item.title: MCP Charts: Add Interactive Charts to Your MCP Server
item.description: Your MCP server returns data. The agent reads it back as a wall of numbers, or it tries to draw a chart in HTML and gets it wrong. The visual, the part the user actually wanted, gets left to the model to improvise. MCP charts fix that. You give your MCP server one visualize tool: the agent calls it...
item.dc:creator: Max Mealing

item.title: How we built our FinOps MCP server: lessons learned
item.description: TL;DR — Our first MCP was basically our API, exposed as-is. Claude wouldn't use it. We had a dedicated tool to compare two periods and it never called it once: it just called query_cost twice and did the subtraction itself. The tools made sense if you knew our database. The model didn't. We ended...
item.dc:creator: Tanguy Compagnon de La Servette

item.title: Beyond text: Returning images and interactive apps from MCP servers
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming a richer foundation for agent experiences. Though most servers return plain text from their tool calls, MCP servers can also return binary results and provide interactive apps in clients that support those features, like VS Code. In this post, I'll use...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis

item.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
item.description: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol
